title: Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 2nd February 2026 | TV5 Money

recommendations.note: Accumulate near 640-650; demerger likely within 6 months
recommendations.detail: He said the Vedanta demerger issue looks set to be completed within the next six months, so one can buy. The correction in non-ferrous metals will not be prolonged because renewable-driven demand will revive non-ferrous metals; Vedanta is one of the larger players. Whenever it closes around Rs 640-650 the stock should be accumulated.

recommendations.note: Hold long term; strong research pipeline, no need to panic
recommendations.detail: A viewer holds Natco Pharma at Rs 950 and asked whether to hold or book profit. Kutumba Rao said hold for the long term with no need to panic, because Natco also has a strong research pipeline.

recommendations.note: Add slowly on every dip in this range; a no-brainer stock
recommendations.detail: Asked about buying for a 1-5+ year horizon given value unlocking, he said one can add slowly here with no trouble. In this price range, keep adding slowly whenever a dip comes. He called it a no-brainer stock.

recommendations.note: Buy at attractive valuation; stock-picker's paradise now
recommendations.detail: Asked by a viewer (Saira Sriram), he said to definitely lock it down and buy, as the stock now has an attractive valuation. He added that with the budget over and only the Indo-US deal ahead, it is now a stock-picker's paradise - favour companies that benefit indirectly from the budget or have completed expansion capex.

recommendations.stock: ICICI Prudential Nifty 200 Fund / Bandhan Infrastructure Fund
recommendations.note: Switch out of broader-market and infra themes into diversified funds
recommendations.detail: A viewer (Nagarajan) was at a loss in ICICI Prudential Nifty 200 (broader-market) and Bandhan Infrastructure (sectoral). Kutumba Rao said negative sentiment is heavier in the broader market versus Nifty 50, and infra is unlikely to see any big move even post-budget. He advised exiting these as far as possible and moving into diversified flexi-cap or multi-cap funds, and warned against chasing gold/silver - asset allocation must be followed strictly.
